questions.question: What does reactive() actually return when you pass it a plain object?
options: A deep clone of the original object
The original object with added non-enumerable properties
A Proxy wrapping the original object
A readonly snapshot of the original object

questions.question: Which utility unwraps a reactive proxy back to the original plain object for identity comparison?
What is the recommended way to track which item is 'selected' from a reactive list to avoid the proxy identity hazard?
options: Store a reference to the reactive proxy object
Use toRaw() on both sides of every comparison
Compare by a unique primitive identifier like an ID string
Use a WeakMap with the reactive objects as keys
